records.title: DNS Database
records.summary: A production Go service for ingesting, resolving, storing, and querying domain, IP, CNAME, and reverse-DNS relationships.
records.search_text: dns database a production go service for ingesting, resolving, storing, and querying domain, ip, cname, and reverse-dns relationships. maintain a durable dns intelligence store that can absorb large file-based inputs, refresh stale records, expose predictable query interfaces, and remain operable under sustained workloads. created and architected nearly the entire dns database system, including its core package structure, storage model, ingestion and resolution flows, transport interfaces, lifecycle behavior, documentation, and test strategy. later commits and maintenance by other contributors do not represent shared creation of the underlying system. modular go service with 50+ tracked test files rocksdb-backed persistent storage domain and reverse-dns file ingestion a, aaaa, and cname resolution workflows unix-socket and rest query interfaces prometheus monitoring and profiling support directory watchers turn domain and reverse-dns files into bounded processing flows. resolver workers enrich records through dns lookups with configurable retries and backoff. rocksdb stores bidirectional domain, ip, cname, blacklist, and tld relationships. rest and unix-socket servers expose the same query model to different consumers. configuration controls worker counts, channel buffers, retention, freshness, storage tuning, and shutdown behavior. coordinate high-volume concurrent work without leaking goroutines or losing completion signals. keep related indexes consistent when records are updated, merged, deleted, or refreshed. separate ingestion, resolution, persistence, transport, and monitoring into testable packages. make long-lived operations observable and safely stoppable. document architecture and operating constraints so the system remains maintainable beyond its original implementation. more than 130 tracked source and support files across server, client, storage, resolver, watcher, monitoring, and configuration packages. unit, integration, race-oriented, and stress-oriented test suites. module-level architecture documentation and a detailed operator readme. containerized build and validation flow with linting, security checks, race detection, coverage, and profiling. maintainability lifecycles documentation architecture is communication maintainability over hype architecture is communication maintainability over hype

records.title: External Playwright Adapter
records.summary: An isolated adapter that lets independently owned Playwright suites participate in shared execution, evidence, and test-management workflows.
records.search_text: external playwright adapter an isolated adapter that lets independently owned playwright suites participate in shared execution, evidence, and test-management workflows. let teams keep specialized tests beside their own code without importing framework internals or weakening the canonical dsl-based release gate. sole designer and implementer of the external playwright adapter, including repository synchronization, path validation, isolated dependency installation, execution aggregation, result mapping, and ci-safe source references. one governed source directory maps to one managed test case repository, ref, and path validation before execution aggregates multiple playwright specs into one managed result synthetic junit output carries source revision and authority metadata dry-run-first synchronization for case metadata external cases are separated before canonical dsl generation begins. the runtime validates a governed repository, revision, relative path, and required spec files. external suites install and use their own dependencies instead of importing framework internals. multiple contained playwright tests are reduced to one authoritative managed-case outcome. synthetic junit records preserve source path, revision, commit, and execution authority. create a removable integration boundary that does not contaminate the core generator. prevent test-management content from selecting arbitrary repositories or escaping configured roots. define deterministic semantics for failed, errored, skipped, and empty external suites. keep metadata synchronization reviewable through dry-run, strict validation, and explicit apply modes. dedicated adapter documentation and focused automated tests. explicit isolation and removal contracts. source revision metadata travels with generated execution evidence. ci and local execution use the same orchestration path. integration boundaries pragmatism test generation platform test generation platform

records.title: JARVIS
records.summary: A personal system for reducing context switching across projects, memory, documentation, automation, and creative work.
records.search_text: jarvis a personal system for reducing context switching across projects, memory, documentation, automation, and creative work. bring the different parts of long-running work into one understandable context while preserving human control at meaningful decision points.  connect projects, memory, and documentation coordinate automation and creative work reduce context switching keep consequential decisions behind explicit gates memory orchestration human agency test generation platform architecture is communication core override test generation platform jarvis alpha architecture is communication core override

records.title: Test Generation Platform
records.summary: A company-wide QA platform that turns reviewable test contracts into deterministic Playwright execution and evidence.
records.search_text: test generation platform a company-wide qa platform that turns reviewable test contracts into deterministic playwright execution and evidence. make release-gate intent understandable to humans while keeping generation, execution, scheduling, and result reporting repeatable enough for ci. sole creator and architect of the playwright qa platform. designed and implemented the generator, runtime orchestration, kiwitcms integration, dsl and selector contracts, ci behavior, evidence handling, scheduling, documentation, and external-case integration. kiwitcms to dsl to ir to playwright pipeline schema-driven command contracts and selector profiles deterministic typescript generation with jinja2 lane-aware parallel execution junit, html, trace, and per-case video evidence shared docker runtime for local, ci, and interactive use kiwitcms holds intended coverage and human-readable case contracts. normalization and schema-driven mapping translate case text into an explicit intermediate representation. jinja2 templates render understandable playwright typescript rather than opaque runtime instructions. profile inheritance composes shared, product-specific, and legacy dsl, selector, and runtime layers. a single containerized entrypoint coordinates export, lint, generation, execution, reporting, traces, and interactive debugging. keep the same input deterministic across normalization, mapping, rendering, and execution. reject ambiguous test language through lint modes, typed selector semantics, and contract tests. support multiple products without copying the generator or weakening the shared schema. schedule shared-user cases safely while preserving parallelism for independent cases. preserve useful evidence and map terminal test outcomes back into the test-management system. roughly 14,000 lines across python, typescript, shell orchestration, templates, and integration tooling. more than 20 focused generator contract-test modules plus integration-library tests. dedicated documentation for operators, stakeholders, dsl authors, schema contracts, selectors, troubleshooting, and adapters. dry-run and strict lint paths allow case authors and ci to validate contracts before execution. quality translation automation external playwright adapter maintainability over hype jarvis external playwright adapter jarvis test generation platform maintainability over hype
